response_summary.first_message_text:
<proposed_plan>
  <game_title>QuickCalc Challenge</game_title>
  <description>A fast-paced, single-player arithmetic game where players solve math problems against a countdown timer to build combos and achieve high scores.</description>
  <core_mechanics>
    <mechanic>Generates random arithmetic problems (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) with configurable difficulty.</mechanic>
    <mechanic>Supports both typed answers and 4-option multiple choice for accessibility.</mechanic>
    <mechanic>Timer bar that depletes per round; correct answers add time, incorrect answers deduct time.</mechanic>
    <mechanic>Combo multiplier rewards consecutive correct answers with bonus points.</mechanic>
  </core_mechanics>
  <ui_ux>
    <element>Centered, large numeric display with high-contrast colors for readability.</element>
    <element>Visual feedback: green pulse for correct, red shake for incorrect.</element>
    <element>HUD shows current score, combo multiplier, timer bar, and level.</element>
    <element>Responsive layout optimized for mobile touch and desktop keyboard/mouse.</element>
  </ui_ux>
  <progression>
    <stage>Levels 1-3: Addition & subtraction (numbers 1-20)</stage>
    <stage>Levels 4-6: Multiplication & division (tables 2-10)</stage>
    <stage>Levels 7+: Mixed operations, larger operands (up to 99), faster decay</stage>
    <stage>Endless mode unlocks after completing level 10</stage>
  </progression>
  <technical_approach>
    <stack>HTML5, CSS3, Vanilla JavaScript (zero dependencies)</stack>
    <state_management>Plain object tracking score, level, timer, combo, and problem queue</state_management>
    <randomization>Weighted problem generator that adapts to player streak and accuracy</randomization>
    <performance>DOM updates batched per tick; requestAnimationFrame for smooth timer animation</performance>
  </technical_approach>
  <next_steps>
    <step>Build math problem generator with difficulty scaling and answer validation</step>
    <step>Implement timer loop, scoring, and combo multiplier logic</step>
    <step>Design and connect UI components (HUD, input fields, feedback animations)</step>
    <step>Conduct cross-device testing, tune difficulty curves, and finalize</step>
  </next_steps>
</proposed_plan>
